title: Kalu Waterfall
description: Kalu Waterfall is the highest and largest waterfall in the Malshej Ghat region, plunging from a massive height of approximately 1,200 feet. Falling in a multi-tiered drop against the rugged cliffs of the Harishchandragad sanctuary, it creates an awe-inspiring mist shroud that makes it a dream destination for extreme trekkers.

title: Devkund Waterfall
bestSeason: Late Monsoon and Winter (September to January)
description: Devkund Waterfall is a pristine plunge waterfall hidden deep within the dense forests of Bhira near Patnus. Widely regarded as one of the most beautiful natural pools in India, the water falls fiercely into a crystal-clear turquoise pool, forming the exact origin point of the Kundalika River.

title: Thoseghar Waterfalls
description: Thoseghar Waterfalls is a magnificent series of cascades located 20 km from Satara at the edge of the Konkan region. Surrounded by a clean, dense green amphitheater, the site features multiple smaller seasonal drops alongside a main, roaring 200-meter vertical waterfall that comes alive during heavy rains.
